title: ochrosia coccinea var. peekelii
intro: Ochrosia coccinea var. peekelii, also known as the Red Ochrosia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to the tropical forests of Papua New Guinea and the surrounding islands. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Ochrosia coccinea var. peekelii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support digestive health, making it a valuable herbal remedy in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
